entries.title: 1896 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The inaugural modern Olympic Games, with 241 athletes from 14 nations competing in 43 events.

entries.title: 1900 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Held as part of the 1900 World's Fair; women competed for the first time.

entries.title: 1904 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games held outside Europe, overshadowed by the concurrent World's Fair.

entries.title: 1908 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The marathon distance of 26 miles 385 yards, run here, later became the standard.

entries.title: 1912 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games to use electronic timing and a public address system.

entries.title: 1920 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first postwar Games, introducing the Olympic flag and the athletes' oath.

entries.title: 1924 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games with a closing ceremony and an official Olympic Village.

entries.title: 1928 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games with a permanently lit Olympic flame.

entries.title: 1932 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Held during the Great Depression, with a podium ceremony introduced for medalists.

entries.title: 1936 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Used by the Nazi regime for propaganda; Jesse Owens won four gold medals.

entries.title: 1948 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games after a 12-year gap caused by the Second World War.

entries.title: 1952 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The Soviet Union competed at the Olympics for the first time.

entries.title: 1956 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games held in the Southern Hemisphere.

entries.title: 1960 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games to be broadcast live on television across Europe.

entries.title: 1964 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games held in Asia, and the first broadcast live via satellite.

entries.title: 1968 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games held in Latin America; Bob Beamon's long jump record stood for 23 years.

entries.title: 1972 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Marked by the hostage-taking and killing of eleven Israeli athletes and coaches.

entries.title: 1976 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Nadia Comăneci scored gymnastics' first perfect 10.

entries.title: 1980 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Boycotted by the United States and dozens of other countries over the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.

entries.title: 1984 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Boycotted by the Soviet Union and allies; the first privately financed Games.

entries.title: 1988 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The last Games of the Cold War era, with broad international participation restored.

entries.title: 1992 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games since 1972 with no boycotts; the US 'Dream Team' debuted in basketball.

entries.title: 1996 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Held for the Games' centennial; a bombing in Centennial Olympic Park killed two people.

entries.title: 2000 Summer Olympics
entries.description: North and South Korean athletes marched together in the opening ceremony.

entries.title: 2004 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Returned the Games to their birthplace 108 years after the first modern Olympics.

entries.title: 2008 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Michael Phelps won a record eight gold medals in a single Games.

entries.title: 2012 Summer Olympics
entries.description: London became the first city to host the Summer Games three times.

entries.title: 2016 Summer Olympics
entries.description: The first Games held in South America.

entries.title: 2020 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Postponed a year by the COVID-19 pandemic and held largely without spectators.

entries.title: 2024 Summer Olympics
entries.description: Paris became the second city, after London, to host the Summer Games three times.

entries.title: 1924 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Originally an 'International Winter Sports Week', retroactively recognized as the first Winter Olympics.

entries.title: 1928 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Warm weather forced the cancellation of several events.

entries.title: 1932 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Held during the Great Depression with only 17 participating nations.

entries.title: 1936 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The last Winter Games before a 12-year wartime hiatus.

entries.title: 1948 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games after the Second World War; Germany and Japan were not invited.

entries.title: 1952 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games held in a Scandinavian country.

entries.title: 1956 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games televised live, and the Soviet Union's first appearance.

entries.title: 1960 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games to use a computer to help tabulate results.

entries.title: 1964 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The Austrian army helped truck in snow after an unusually mild winter.

entries.title: 1968 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games with widespread color television coverage.

entries.title: 1972 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games held in Asia.

entries.title: 1976 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Innsbruck stepped in as host after Denver withdrew following a local referendum.

entries.title: 1980 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The US ice hockey team's 'Miracle on Ice' upset the Soviet Union.

entries.title: 1984 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games held in a communist and a Balkan country.

entries.title: 1988 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Expanded to 16 days, the longest Winter Games to that point.

entries.title: 1992 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The last Winter Games held in the same year as the Summer Games.

entries.title: 1994 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The first Winter Games held on its own 4-year cycle, offset from the Summer Games.

entries.title: 1998 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Women's ice hockey debuted as an Olympic event.

entries.title: 2002 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Held five months after the September 11 attacks, under heightened security.

entries.title: 2006 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Set a record at the time for the number of participating nations at a Winter Games.

entries.title: 2010 Winter Olympics
entries.description: A luge athlete died in a training crash on the day of the opening ceremony.

entries.title: 2014 Winter Olympics
entries.description: The most expensive Olympics ever held, at an estimated cost of over $50 billion.

entries.title: 2018 Winter Olympics
entries.description: North and South Korea marched together and fielded a joint women's ice hockey team.

entries.title: 2022 Winter Olympics
entries.description: Beijing became the first city to host both the Summer and Winter Olympics.
